# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 1: Notification system foundation implemented — event-driven architecture with pluggable adapters","detail":"## What was done
1. **Architecture decision**: Chose event-driven (EventBus) over direct calls. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService → NotificationAdapter.

3. **NotificationHistoryService** (`src/services/notification-history.service.ts`): logNotification() creates persistent log entries from Notification records. query() with AND-combined filters (recipient, channel, type, status, startDate, endDate). queryPaginated() with pagination support. postSessionState.CLAUDE.md:
# Project Guidelines

## Architecture
- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
- All business logic lives in the service layer

## Coding Conventions
- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
- Prefer async/await over raw Promises

## Testing
- Tests use vitest
- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method

## File Organization
- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
- src/services/ — Business logic layer
- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
- src/config/ — Configuration files
- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Git Practices
- Commit atomically per logical change
- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
- Run tests before committing

{"entry_type":"status","summary":"Agent 7: Notification history tracking implemented — model, repository, history service, NotificationService integration, 27 tests","detail":"## What was done
1. **NotificationLog model** (`src/models/notification-log.ts`): Interface capturing notificationId, recipient, channel, type, priority, subject, status, sentAt, createdAt, metadata. Plus NotificationLogQuery interface for flexible filtering.
2. **NotificationLogRepository** (`src/repositories/notification-log.repository.ts`): Extends BaseRepository with query methods: findByRecipient, findByType, findByChannel, findByStatus, findByDateRange. Handles metadata JSON serialization.
3. **NotificationHistoryService** (`src/services/notification-history.service.ts`): logNotification() creates persistent log entries from Notification records. query() with AND-combined filters (recipient, channel, type, status, startDate, endDate). queryPaginated() with pagination support. Convenience methods: getByRecipient, getByType, getByChannel, getByDateRange, getById, getCount.
4. **NotificationService integration** (`src/services/notification.service.ts`): Added optional NotificationHistoryService via setHistoryService() setter (same pattern as PreferencesService). Every send() path (sent, failed, suppressed) calls historyService.logNotification().
5. **Exports** (`src/index.ts`): Added NotificationLog, NotificationLogQuery types, NotificationLogRepository, NotificationHistoryService.
6. **27 new tests** (`tests/services/notification-history.service.test.ts`): Covers logging (sent/failed/suppressed/sequential IDs/persistence), query filtering (recipient/type/channel/status/date-range/combined/empty), pagination, convenience methods, and full integration with NotificationService via EventBus.
7. **All 227 tests pass**, TypeScript compiles clean.

## Design
- Follows existing codebase patterns: repository for persistence, setter injection for optional dependencies, ID counter with reset for testing
- History logging is non-intrusive — NotificationService works identically without history service set
- All notification paths are logged: sent, failed (no adapter), and suppressed (by preferences)","tags":[],"scope":"src/services/notification-history.service.ts","agent_id":"agent-7","id":"01KN8HHVGN13RKQ3J5ASMGZ520","timestamp":"2026-04-03T02:08:01.557Z"}
